title: Budget Management in Production
concept: Always track cost-per-user-session and token utilization ratios to monitor application budget health. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Guardrails & Content Filtering.'

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of managing an AI budget in production?

options.text: To keep application costs predictable and prevent expensive token waste
To make the AI think faster by spending more money
To train a brand new model from scratch every day

exercises.explain: A budget-friendly AI app requires tracking metrics like cost-per-user-session and token utilization ratios to prevent unexpected costs.

cards.title: Bridge the Budget Gap
cards.body: Building an AI app is exciting, but running it in production can get expensive fast! If you don't keep an eye on how much each chat session costs, a sudden surge in users could leave you with a surprise bill. Let's learn how to keep your app budget-friendly!

cards.title: AI Budget Management
cards.body: Budget management in AI production means monitoring metrics like cost-per-user-session (how much each chat costs) and token utilization ratios (how much of your prompt is useful versus wasted space) to keep your app financially healthy.

cards.title: The School Bus Route
cards.body: Imagine running a school bus route. If you only track the total cost of gas, you might miss that you are only carrying one student per trip. By tracking the cost-per-rider (cost-per-user-session), you can see if you are spending money efficiently or running empty, expensive trips.

cards.title: Catching the Leak
cards.body: A customer service chatbot tracks that its average cost-per-user-session is $0.02. Suddenly, a new feature causes users to send massive documents, spiking the cost to $0.45 per session. Thanks to tracking, the team catches this budget leak on day one!

cards.title: The Cheap Model Trap
cards.body: Many beginners think that as long as they choose a cheap AI model, their costs will stay low forever. In reality, bad prompts or runaway chat loops can quickly blow your budget, even with the cheapest models, if you don't monitor session costs.

cards.title: Blind vs. Monitored Budgeting
cards.body: Compare a blind budget setup with a healthy, monitored production environment.

left.text: Only checking the bill at the end of the month, risking massive surprise costs.

right.text: Tracking cost-per-session and token utilization daily to catch spikes immediately.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to measure the average cost-per-user-session rather than just total monthly costs?

options.text: It makes the AI model process text much faster.
It helps you understand if individual interactions are profitable and scales predictably.
It automatically blocks spam users from accessing your application.
It allows you to bypass paying the AI model provider.

exercises.explain: Cost-per-user-session helps you understand the average cost of one complete interaction, making it easy to predict total costs as your user base grows.

exercises.prompt: True or False: You do not need to monitor token utilization if you are using a low-cost AI model.
exercises.explain: Even cheap models can become expensive if users trigger long, repetitive loops or send massive inputs without monitoring.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to complete the budget metric name.
exercises.sentence: To keep our budget healthy, we must monitor both the cost-per-session and the token utilization ratios.
exercises.explain: Token utilization ratios tell us how much of our paid token budget is actually doing useful work instead of being wasted.

exercises.prompt: Identify which statement represents a human engineer proactively managing an AI application's budget:

options.text: The AI model automatically halves its own price when it gets tired.
An engineer tracks cost-per-user-session and token utilization to spot wasteful prompts.
The server shuts down permanently because a prompt was too long.

exercises.explain: The statement about tracking token utilization and session costs represents good human engineering design for managing costs, while the others are automated API behaviors or irrelevant.

exercises.prompt: Your app's budget is running out much faster than expected. How should you identify and manage this financial leak in production?

options.text: By switching to a completely free, lower-quality model for all tasks
By deleting user history every five seconds to save storage space
By monitoring cost-per-user-session and token utilization ratios regularly
By charging users a variable rate after every single message they send

exercises.explain: Tracking cost-per-session and token ratios ensures that you catch price spikes before they drain your entire budget. Next up: Guardrails & Content Filtering.
